questions.question: The law of readiness is based upon a principle of learning which states that people learn best when:

choices.text: they have the proper environment for learning.
the need for the learning is adequately explained.
the instructor is ready to teach.

questions.question: A disadvantage of using presentation programs is:

choices.text: the potential for over-reliance on technology.
the carousels take up a lot of storage space.
they are difficult and expensive to produce.

questions.correctChoiceText: the potential for over-reliance on technology.

questions.question: A advantage of using handouts as instructional media is:

choices.text: to provide note guides with an outline of the lesson for students to fill in notes during class.
they may be distributed at the beginning of a class for homework assignments.
they can be three-hole punched so they are not lost.

questions.correctChoiceText: to provide note guides with an outline of the lesson for students to fill in notes during class.

questions.question: To gain a clear and accurate evaluation of students' learning during a course, the instructor should:

choices.text: administer written, oral, and performance tests.

questions.correctChoiceText: administer written, oral, and performance tests.

questions.question: An active process that produces a change in behavior as a result of acquiring new information best describes:
When troubleshooting a problem with a multimedia system, you should first:

choices.text: call tech support or another instructor for help.
go to the contingency plan immediately.
think about the problem and potential solutions.
use the help files contained found in software programs.

questions.correctChoiceText: think about the problem and potential solutions.

questions.question: If you decide to use PowerPoint slides, which other equipment will you need to give the presentation?
Resources that can be used to reveal safety problems in need of corrective action are:

choices.text: the number of emergency responses made daily.

questions.question: The more true to life the learning situation, the greater the change in student behavior is the Law of:
The basic instructional method to use in teaching a skill is:
When preparing to give a lecture, you attempt to turn on your projector and it will not turn on. The first thing you should check is that the:

choices.text: projector is connected to the APU.
projector is plugged into the wall.
lens cover is off the projector.

questions.correctChoiceText: projector is plugged into the wall.

questions.question: The primary responsibility for controlling the learning environment belongs to the:
Regardless of the type of training provided, ___ should be a top priority as part of risk management.
Observing and measuring student performance testing should be documented:

choices.text: on a checklist using standard criteria.
by peer students based on lessons learned.
by at least three Instructors as students perform in a group.
visually by the instructor based on their expertise.

questions.correctChoiceText: on a checklist using standard criteria.

questions.question: An attribute of an effective instructor is:
With respect to transitions and animation software, new Instructors have a tendency to:
The law prohibiting discrimination that creates a hostile work environment is the:

choices.text: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act.

questions.correctChoiceText: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act.

questions.question: Adapting a lesson plan:

choices.text: should be done for the purpose of modification.
can significantly change the class or alter the learning objectives.
need not include documentation of the adaptations made.
allows for a specific use or situation.

questions.correctChoiceText: allows for a specific use or situation.

questions.question: Which types of records or information must be kept confidential?

choices.text: Incident or NFIRS (National Fire Incident Reporting System) reports

questions.question: Which training activities may require requesting additional instructors to observe safety related details?

choices.text: A single-company drill reviewing water supply options
A small-company discussion of fireground tactics
Any training session characterized by increased hazards

questions.correctChoiceText: Any training session characterized by increased hazards

questions.question: Which refers to students with dysphasia?

choices.text: Problems with auditory and visual perception
Lack ability to write or spell or place words together to complete a sentence
Problems in reading such as word recognition and comprehension

questions.correctChoiceText: Lack ability to write or spell or place words together to complete a sentence

questions.question: The accuracy of ___ is essential to a successful training program.
Under what circumstance can the fire department refuse to make accommodations for a person with a physical disability?

choices.text: If the accommodation could cause a direct threat to another's health or safety
There is no acceptable circumstance for the fire department to refuse to make accommodations.
If the accommodation costs are too high

questions.correctChoiceText: If the accommodation could cause a direct threat to another's health or safety

questions.question: Hollow square arrangement, is best suited for the delivery of a(n):
Which statement regarding completion test item grading is true?

choices.text: Quality of students' handwriting is never a problem with supply-completion test items.
Supply-completion test items are considered objective.
Selection-completion test items require a list of synonyms as possible correct answers.
Supply-completion test items require manual scoring.

questions.correctChoiceText: Supply-completion test items require manual scoring.

questions.question: The single most important method for determining the competency of actual task performance is:
A major consideration when performing a skill is to demonstrate the ___ way.
According to Houle, which are types of adult learners?
When preparing for a presentation, the instructor must consider logistical support prior to the class and:

choices.text: logistical time to purchase the equipment.
classroom time to refill SCBA bottles after use.
cost associated with replacing spent resources from class activities.
logistical time to develop an evaluation tool.

questions.correctChoiceText: classroom time to refill SCBA bottles after use.

questions.question: A student/instructor relationship based upon mutual respect, confidence, and rapport is:

choices.text: important as a positive attribute for learning.
if the instructor is thoroughly qualified.
not as important if the instructor outranks the students.
not as important if the students outrank the instructor.

questions.correctChoiceText: important as a positive attribute for learning.

questions.question: Training props that use flammable/combustible liquids must have:
The consistent way a person gathers and processes information is called:
Before using any training audiovisual aids, the instructor must:

choices.text: introduce it properly to the group.
determine the best time to use the aid.

questions.question: An effective Instructor I should have experience in:
A duty performed by the instructor during a written test is:

choices.text: ensuring that the environment remains quiet and safe for the students.
arriving ten minutes prior to start of test.
ensuring that all cellular phones, PDAs, and pagers are placed on top of the desk.

questions.correctChoiceText: ensuring that the environment remains quiet and safe for the students.

questions.question: The VARK Preferences is an instrument used by an instructor:

choices.text: to help students identify how they prefer to learn.
identify weaknesses in the curriculum package.
to help the instructors identify their strengths and weaknesses.

questions.correctChoiceText: to help students identify how they prefer to learn.

questions.question: Liability that is placed on the employer for the acts and omissions of instructors during the normal course of employment is:
Training records and reports are necessary to document:

choices.text: and justify the competency of lead instructors.

questions.question: One of the levels of in-service drills is:
When given a topic for instructional delivery, an instructor should determine what materials are needed:

choices.text: by consulting agency policies and procedures.
from the lesson plan on the topic.

questions.correctChoiceText: from the lesson plan on the topic.

questions.question: A factor that affects the learner's ability to learn in the classroom includes:

choices.text: how the course material relates to the real world.
the learner's current position in his/her own development cycle.
the learner's attitude toward the material.
the learner's psychological state of mind.

questions.correctChoiceText: how the course material relates to the real world.

questions.question: The part of the lesson plan that deals with out-of-class requirements that the student would need to complete is the:
Live fire training in an acquired structure is:
The administration of a test begins:

choices.text: at the moment the instructions are given to the students.
at the moment the instructor says: "Begin."
at the moment the test materials are handed out.

questions.question: For a printed article to be considered public domain, it must have been published before:
Labeling the lesson plan with terms such as beginner or advanced references the:
Which is the most important consideration for an instructor when adapting a lesson plan?

choices.text: Making the plan usable for your class
Evaluating the revised plan's safety implications
Making adjustments to meet the instructor's needs
Making adjustments to fit local conditions

questions.correctChoiceText: Evaluating the revised plan's safety implications

questions.question: Negative feedback is:

choices.text: relevant to the action, behavior, or knowledge.
specific on what is correct or incorrect.
directed at the individual and not the skill or behavior.
focused on the action and not the student.

questions.correctChoiceText: directed at the individual and not the skill or behavior.

questions.question: A rule of thumb is that the level of personal protective equipment required for training exercises is:

choices.text: optional as determined by the instructor.
a minimum standard set by the National Fire Protection Association.
the level of PPE that should be worn during an emergency.

questions.correctChoiceText: the level of PPE that should be worn during an emergency.

questions.question: The process of providing for the future leadership of a department or organization is called:
The greatest concern in the outdoor classroom environment is:
The type of chart used by an organization to identify individuals within the department is known as a(n):
When administering a written exam, you should be concerned with:
What is the term used to define what an employer does to assist an individual with a disability covered by Americans with Disabilities Act?
A communication style that involves experienced students and encourages them to incorporate their knowledge and experiences into the learning process is:
The reference section of a lesson plan should include:

choices.text: names of books and other instructional resources.

questions.correctChoiceText: names of books and other instructional resources.

questions.question: Learning will be most effective when a feeling of satisfaction or reward accompanies the learning process is the:
Which statement about examination feedback is correct?

choices.text: It is provided at the convenience of the instructor.
Mistakes and incorrect answers should be reviewed.
It is not important to the learning process.

questions.correctChoiceText: Mistakes and incorrect answers should be reviewed.

questions.question: Which of the following statements concerning the scoring of final performance tests is true?

choices.text: Scoring performance tests are highly objective.
Test evaluators should insert subjective comments about students.
Use multiple test evaluators to observe each student during the test.

questions.correctChoiceText: Use multiple test evaluators to observe each student during the test.

questions.question: A learner can gain more knowledge by:

choices.text: using pure lecture as a medium of learning.
using as many of the senses as practical.

questions.correctChoiceText: using as many of the senses as practical.

questions.question: Which statement regarding test administration is true?

choices.text: Never leave the testing environment unless it is absolutely necessary.
Remain objective with all test takers.
Arrive no sooner than 15 minutes prior to the beginning time for the written test.
Oral tests are generally given in conjunction with written tests.

questions.correctChoiceText: Remain objective with all test takers.

questions.question: Performance standards for fire instructors are identified in:
Which file format could be associated with the Quicktime media programs?
For learning-oriented adults,

choices.text: they acquire knowledge with the intention of improving their job prospects
the content is less important than the act of being involved in the learning.
the learning takes place by doing.
the learning takes place by doing.

questions.correctChoiceText: the content is less important than the act of being involved in the learning.

questions.question: The learning environment known as classroom style is best suited for the delivery of a:
The instructor attribute that brings determination and energy to the training environment is:
In the past, most fire service training took place:
It is the responsibility of an instructor to plan teaching methods so that ideas are conveyed to the learner:

choices.text: through as many of the senses as practical.

questions.correctChoiceText: through as many of the senses as practical.

questions.question: Which is an effective mannerism of the instructor?
A key factor in the classroom environment is the ___ of both instructor and student.
When teaching a new skill set, the instructor, with regard to conflict resolution, should:

choices.text: debate the new policy and question the policy decisions.
listen only to the side of the students.

questions.question: A narrative report includes:

choices.text: a chronological statement of events or activities.

questions.question: As an instructor, you write an article that gets published) The article will be protected under the 1976 Copyright Act for life plus ___ years.
Adjustments to presentations:

choices.text: should not change the learning objectives
requires the instructor to alter the test items.
always modify the content of the lesson.

questions.correctChoiceText: should not change the learning objectives

questions.question: When selecting slides, you should limit the words to ___ lines with no more than ___ words in each.
Students may reach a plateau of learning; in this case one solution is to:

choices.text: provide a deadline by when they must master the skill.
stop and take a break to give students time to think about the task.
immediately test the students to challenge their recall ability.
repeat the lecture portion of the lesson.

questions.correctChoiceText: stop and take a break to give students time to think about the task.

questions.question: Failure by an instructor to correct improper skill performance on the training ground can lead to poor:
Which verb describes an action the instructor must take to create student desire for learning?

Directions: Read the following statements regarding instructors, then choose the appropriate answer from choices A-D below: Statement 1: An instructor needs special training that leads to a teaching certificate. Statement 2: Instructors must have a desire to understand the needs of learners. Statement 3: Instructors must be fair and impartial to all students.

choices.text: Statements 1 and 2 are false; statement 3 is true.
Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.
Statements 1 and 2 are false; statement 3 is true.

questions.correctChoiceText: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.

questions.question: When using a PowerPoint presentation, you should:

choices.text: keystone the display to ensure accurate information.
turn off the lights so everyone can read the slides.
stand off to the side of the screen while discussing the slide.
read from the slide to be accurate.

questions.correctChoiceText: stand off to the side of the screen while discussing the slide.

questions.question: Instructors can adjust their presentation to maximize learning outcomes by using:

choices.text: the same presentation style to maintain consistency.
a variety of presentations to cover a variety of learning domains.
lectures with carefully selected audio visual examples.

questions.correctChoiceText: a variety of presentations to cover a variety of learning domains.

questions.question: Frequently, instructors overlook the importance of ___ when they conduct classes in recreation rooms, apparatus rooms, or dormitories.
A lesson plan format includes:
A relatively permanent change in behavior potential that is traceable to experience and practice is a definition for:
To overcome subjectivity in performance testing, the training division should:

choices.text: have multiple instructors validate skill checklists.
document rationale and allow instructors flexibility.

questions.correctChoiceText: have multiple instructors validate skill checklists.

questions.question: A proper learning environment for classroom and drill ground activities requires:

choices.text: student selection of personal protective gear.
actual hazards to aid in realism.

questions.question: In the Four-Step Method of Instruction, when is a learner given an opportunity to practice what has been learned?
Whether the training is academic or practical, success during the presentation step requires thorough planning and should:

choices.text: always proceed from the known to the unknown.

questions.question: The purpose of performance checklists based on objectives is to:

choices.text: identify to the evaluator the rationale for student performance.
document subjective impressions by the evaluator.

questions.question: In the learning environment, the student is the:
Which would usually be given consideration during the session preparation phase?

Directions: Read the following statements and choose your answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: The type and format of the training records must be standardized from agency to agency. Statement 2: Records document facts, while reports summarize facts contained in the records. Statement 3: Instructors are required to know the current legal requirements for record keeping.

choices.text: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.
Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.
Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.

questions.correctChoiceText: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.

questions.question: A duty performed by the instructor before administering a written test is to:

choices.text: ensure that students are not cheating.
ensure that the environment remains quiet and safe for the students.
turn off and put away all electronic devices.
supply extra paper and answer sheets.

questions.correctChoiceText: turn off and put away all electronic devices.

questions.question: Which statement is correct?

choices.text: Eliminating noise distractions outside is very easy.
Control of classroom lighting is an important part of the learning environment.
Handouts are rarely used by students.
Small group activities have limited use in the fire service.

questions.correctChoiceText: Control of classroom lighting is an important part of the learning environment.

questions.question: The best way to handle a failure of audio-visual equipment is to:

choices.text: move to the next course section.

questions.question: Disruptive individuals can be managed by:

choices.text: embarrassing the individual who distracts the class.
calling on the student and redirecting their energy.

questions.correctChoiceText: calling on the student and redirecting their energy.

questions.question: Usually, test results for a student are released or reported to the:
Which is part of the Evaluation Step of the instructional process?

choices.text: Having the learner perform the skills unassisted
Using textbooks and other reference materials

questions.correctChoiceText: Having the learner perform the skills unassisted

questions.question: The method of instruction used when you want learners to get practical experience without real life risks is:
How should an instructor adapt prepared instructional materials to accommodate low literacy level students?

choices.text: Write with cursive when utilizing easel pads or marker boards
Require them to take notes so they will have better retention

questions.question: Each fire department has its own way of doing buisiness which is often referred to as a department's:
It is advisable to permit gifted learners to:

choices.text: assist you in the course when working with a slow student.

questions.correctChoiceText: assist you in the course when working with a slow student.

questions.question: The lesson summary component of a lesson plan refers to:

choices.text: readings or research to be completed outside the classroom.
the pace of presenting the lesson plan.
the conclusion of the lesson to reinforce the main points.
previous requirements that must have been met.

questions.correctChoiceText: the conclusion of the lesson to reinforce the main points.

questions.question: Which is a function of the instructor regarding management of resources for a training program?

choices.text: Administer a budget for training functions.
Adjust the resources, staff, facilities, and training program budget.
A full understanding of the training goals of the department.

questions.correctChoiceText: A full understanding of the training goals of the department.

questions.question: The definition of training is:

choices.text: knowledge-based learning objectives that are not tied to a specific job.
instruction that emphasizes job-specific learning objectives and skills-based instruction.
relatively permanent and observable change in a person's behavior.
method of transferring knowledge by giving information through various forms of communication.

questions.correctChoiceText: instruction that emphasizes job-specific learning objectives and skills-based instruction.

questions.question: During the administration of a written examination, you observer a student cheating on the exam. You should:

choices.text: move them to the front of the room and allow them to finish the test.
allow them to continue testing and make note of their activities.
know and follow your department's policity.
excuse them from the examination room and destroy their test.

questions.correctChoiceText: know and follow your department's policity.

questions.question: Which is a normal indicator of learning disabilities in the students?

choices.text: Difficutly performing fine or precise psychomotor skills

questions.correctChoiceText: Difficutly performing fine or precise psychomotor skills

questions.question: In Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs, the level that deals with people who feel profound moments of accomplishment is:
An instructor should use the ___ presentation method to reach a large audience.
The instructional sequencing method that starts with an overview of a topic, then discusses each individual topic and finishes with a review of the main point is called:
In a training situation, the instructor I should:

choices.text: keep cost under budget to ensure continued funding.
put course objectives ahead of outcome assessments.
put safety ahead of course objectives.

questions.correctChoiceText: put safety ahead of course objectives.

questions.question: The last step in conducting a skills performance evalutaion is to:

choices.text: discuss the results with the instructor.
record the results of the exam.
provide results to the designated person.

questions.correctChoiceText: provide results to the designated person.

questions.question: Which information is usually kept as part of an agency's training records?
You are preparing to teach an Self-Contained Breathing Apparatus (SCBA) class. For which issue would you normally be responsible?

choices.text: Reviewing the lesson plan and assembling the audio/visual aids
Selecting the type of SCBA for your department
Establishing a budget for the class

questions.correctChoiceText: Reviewing the lesson plan and assembling the audio/visual aids

questions.question: When administering an oral test, instructors should:

choices.text: write the responses down and grade them based on professional opinion.
write subjective comments about the individual's oral responses.

questions.question: Which of the following duties is performed by the fire instructor during administration of a performance test?

choices.text: Ensuring that the subject matter of the test matches the subject matter that is being tested
Recording students' scores on performance checklists
Providing students with adequate practice time before the test begins

questions.correctChoiceText: Recording students' scores on performance checklists

questions.question: According to the applicable NFPA Standard, there are ___ defined levels of fire service instructors.
One accepted method for lesson plan delivery consists of:
Which statement regarding lesson plan adaptation is true for an instructor?

choices.text: An Instructor I can make basic or fundamental changes to a lesson plan.
Adapting a lesson plan includes making changes to the learning objective.
An Instructor I can make a lesson plan fit the learning environment.
A lesson plan is routinely implemented exactly as written.

questions.correctChoiceText: An Instructor I can make a lesson plan fit the learning environment.

questions.question: A reason for an instructor to modify a lesson plan is:

choices.text: lack of confidence in a subject area.

questions.question: Which is an indicator of a learning disability?

choices.text: Problems in mathematical areas such as calculating and reasoning
Disadvantages created by economic or enviornmental factors

questions.correctChoiceText: Problems in mathematical areas such as calculating and reasoning

questions.question: In addition to delivering instructional sessions using prepared course materials, the Instructor I:

choices.text: develops instructional materials for specific topics.
presents lessons from prepared lesson plans.

questions.correctChoiceText: presents lessons from prepared lesson plans.

questions.question: Which of the following is true regarding vertical keystoning of the projector?

choices.text: Lines are parallel yet the image looks out of focus.
It occurs when the projector is at a less than 75 degree angle with screen.
Keystoning creates a distraction for the students.
It occurs when the width of the projected image is wider at the bottom than the top.

questions.correctChoiceText: Keystoning creates a distraction for the students.

questions.question: When conducting an outside demonstration, medium to large training props can include:
The instructor gains the students' attention in which step?
Which statement is the most correct regarding the legal aspects of training records?

choices.text: Individual training records are in the public domain.
Acts and omission laws provide immunity for instructors.
Instructors should provide "off the record" reports.

questions.question: A good seating arrangement when instructing a class composed of a small discussion group is:

choices.text: small rows to prevent eye contact.

questions.question: Which is considered a multimedia component?
Responsibility for inspecting equipment prior to being used during a training evolution is the responsibility of the:
The purpose of a keynote speaker is to ___, whereas an instructor's purpose is to ___.
Demonstrating that you are listening and have understood the message is:
Speech communication professionals recommend two types of transitions:
The ability to use several different communication styles during the same presentation is a form of ___ communication.

Directions: Read the following statements and choose the correct answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: To ensure safe training environments, instructors must inspect, maintain, and repair training facilities, props, tools, and equipment. Statement 2: The authority having jurisdiction should establish an inspection time schedule based on industry practice, manufacturer's recommendations, and local needs. Statement 3: Inspections do not need to occur before every scenario.

choices.text: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.
Statement 1 is true; statements 2 and 3 are false.
Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.

questions.correctChoiceText: Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.

questions.question: The Copyright Act allows instructors to:

choices.text: copy up to 50 copies for free.
prevent copying materials for use in the classroom.

questions.question: Which is a method of assistance instructors can provide to students with learning disabilities?

choices.text: Demonstrations in front of the class

questions.question: Safety is the highest priority when training; it is imperitive that what is taught be:

choices.text: required by the National Fire Protection Association (NFPA).
emphasized with bold type in the lesson plan.
consistent written safety policies and procedures.
regulated by the Federal Emergency Management Association (FEMA) standards.

questions.correctChoiceText: consistent written safety policies and procedures.

questions.question: Which could create the perception of communcation barriers that is a major impediment to successful leadership?
Intructors can redirect disruptive students by:

choices.text: embarrassing them in front of their peers.
having the person report to their supervisor.

questions.question: Which is a reason for accurate training records?

choices.text: Employee benefits are often based on training records.
Training records can be used in a legal proceeding.
Inaccurate records provide justification for more instructors.
Training records are considered property of the training agency.

questions.correctChoiceText: Training records can be used in a legal proceeding.

questions.question: Coaching will include:

choices.text: being a follower rather than a leader.

questions.question: Which information should be identified on the skill checklist used for performance testing?
A test that compares a learner's performance against a predetermined standard is known as a ___ test.
Which is a type of record or document that can be used to document training?
Test scores should be released:

choices.text: to each individual in a secure manner.
in a form letter to all students.
buy posting on a bulletin board.
on a group basis, at the final class session.

questions.correctChoiceText: to each individual in a secure manner.

questions.question: When providing feedback on testing materials, the instructor should:

choices.text: review the test with the class.
post names and scores in chronological order.
counsel the low scoring students with the class present.

questions.correctChoiceText: review the test with the class.

questions.question: For new instructor to become proficient at using multiple types of media, they should:

choices.text: be familiar and practice using the media.
not use the media if they don't know how.
request other instructors to operate equipment.

questions.correctChoiceText: be familiar and practice using the media.

questions.question: A general characteristic of a Gen-Y is they:

choices.text: use technology as a part of their daily lives.
place a high value on fairness and hard work.

questions.correctChoiceText: use technology as a part of their daily lives.

questions.question: An important part of computer maintenance is:

choices.text: obtaining a written contract covering terms of repair.
the use of current presentation software.
having an expert use the equipment.

questions.correctChoiceText: obtaining a written contract covering terms of repair.

questions.question: In a skills demonstration, the first thing the instructor should do is:

choices.text: perform the skill at normal speed.
have a learner perform the skill while the instructor explains the steps.
provide an explanation of the skill.
perform the skill slowly so learners can observe all steps.

questions.correctChoiceText: provide an explanation of the skill.

questions.question: Which statement is true?

choices.text: The fire service is exempt from providing specialized attention.
Different ability levels do not effect the amount of instructor time.
Some students may require tutoring or individual instruction.
Students who are quick to learn always require less instructor time.

questions.correctChoiceText: Some students may require tutoring or individual instruction.

questions.question: The description of minimum acceptable behaviors that a student must display is:
A written test that may be difficult to score because it requires a brief factual answer is the:
Which concern is most important to the fire service and the learner?
Which is most commonly encountered in the performance of otherwise lawful acts?
Developing a report narrative depends on providing answers to ___ questions that begin with the letter "w."
The instructor provides opportunity for students to learn through activities during which part of the Four-Step Method of Instruction?
___ are an effective means of providing students with materials to take home.
The flesch-Kincaid Readability Index is:

choices.text: used to measure the reading level of students.
based on the number words read in one minute.
based on the average number of words per sentence and the number of syllables per 100 words.
based on the average number of syllables used per sentence.

questions.correctChoiceText: based on the average number of words per sentence and the number of syllables per 100 words.

questions.question: As you review the lesson plan for a class you have been asked to teach, you notice that some of the learning objectives are no longer needed because of an equipment change. As an Instructor I you should next:

choices.text: notify your supervisor, so that the lesson plan can be modified.
rewrite the learning objectives so they apply to the new equipment.
delete the unnecessary objectives from the lesson plan.

questions.correctChoiceText: notify your supervisor, so that the lesson plan can be modified.

questions.question: Presenting a topic and discussion in an orderly exchange is:
Which statement is true for an instructor?

choices.text: The one area that may not be readily modified is the method of instruction.
Ensure that the adaptations are not really modifications.
Reviewing and adapting a lesson plan should be an informal process.
Adaptations of a lesson plan do not need to be documented.

questions.correctChoiceText: Ensure that the adaptations are not really modifications.

questions.question: In the teaching/learning situation, humor should be used:

choices.text: when the lead instructor is not present.

questions.question: Students with learning disabilities should:

choices.text: have all their tests provided verbally.
have to complete prerequisite work to prove their eligibility.

questions.question: Tabletop models are intended to:

choices.text: be the same size as the prototype.
show the cross-section of a piece of equipment.
show operating principles in a simulated incident.

questions.correctChoiceText: show operating principles in a simulated incident.

questions.question: Your supervisor provides you with the lesson plan for a course you are to teach in two weeks. Which should be your next action?

choices.text: Begin creating you own lesson plan and compare it to the one you were give.
Safely store the lesson plan away until the day of the class.
Review the lesson plan you received and prepare a timeline for the class.

questions.correctChoiceText: Review the lesson plan you received and prepare a timeline for the class.

questions.question: Instructors should slow their speaking speed when:
The time frame component of a lesson plan refers to:

choices.text: conclusions of the lesson to clarify uncertainties.
pace of presenting the lesson plan.
readings or research to be completed outside the classroom.
previous requirements that a student must have mastered.

questions.correctChoiceText: pace of presenting the lesson plan.

questions.question: An instructor should give challenging assignments to:
When weather conditions are hot and humid, the instructor may assess the available options by:

choices.text: waiting until the session is complete before rehab.
keeping students in full personal protective equipment.

questions.correctChoiceText: being a visionary leader and monitoring future trends in the fire service.

questions.question: The information gained by using a pre-course quiz or survey from students can be used for:
